title: scaraboides manningii
intro: Scaraboides manningii, also known as the Australian Mint Bush, is a small shrub native to the eastern coast of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its leaves. The leaves of the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have also been used to aid in wound healing and to reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Scaraboides manningii are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
